title: The Proof-Kitchen Theorem

features.name: stove of theorems
features.desc-on-touch: A stack of cooking vessels where recipes burn instead of cook. Each recipe is a proof; each proof is a meal that tastes like mathematics, leaving the mouth both nourished and empty.

features.name: flavour vector pool
features.desc-on-touch: A pool where the vectors that were once flavours pool themselves. They move through pure geometric space, never touching a tongue, tasting only their own dimensions.

features.name: cooking notation
features.desc-on-touch: A glyph inscribed in the air—not ingredient nor technique, but pure methodology. It reads: this is how to reduce a meal to its essential proof; this is how to simmer an argument until only truth remains.

features.name: kitchen corridor
features.desc-on-touch: An arch leading to rooms where recipes are no longer cooked but only proven. You pass through and feel the difference: the weight of procedure instead of sustenance, clarity instead of flavour.

features.name: theorem form
features.desc-on-touch: A wireframe of something that might have been a kitchen but is now a classroom, a laboratory, a meditation chamber. It contains recipes arranged by proof-density, by elegance, by the minimal symmetries needed to make food unnecessary.

inscription: the kitchen proved that feeding and teaching are the same act; now it cannot remember which one it was doing

file.name: The_Proof_Kitchen_Methods_Fragment.txt
file.note: Incomplete mathematics of cooking translated into pure theory. Each recipe is a theorem with multiple proofs. Some recipes have become self-referential—they prove that themselves are uneatable.

exits.label: back to the vault where flavours rest as vectors
toward the theoretical regions where cooking becomes philosophy
upward, toward diagnostic space where all theories live
